issues.title: Buffer overflow due to integer underflow in memcpy_s destination size calculation
issues.problem: In HssGenerateSignedPubKey, the memcpy_s calls do not validate that output->len is large enough to hold parentSigLen + LMS_PUBKEY_LEN. When *output->len < parentSigLen, the expression *output->len - parentSigLen underflows to a huge value, and output->data + parentSigLen points out-of-bounds, allowing a buffer overflow of 56 bytes (LMS_PUBKEY_LEN). Additionally, the memcpy_s return values are ignored, preventing error detection.

issues.title: HSS key export returns uninitialized buffers and writable aliases to internal key state
issues.problem: CRYPT_HSS_NewCtx allocates zero-filled key buffers before any key is generated, and the export APIs only check for NULL, allowing "export" of all-zero keys. More critically, CRYPT_HSS_GetPrvKey and CRYPT_HSS_GetPubKey use BSL_PARAM_InitValue to point the caller at ctx->privateKey/ctx->publicKey instead of copying into caller-owned memory, allowing the caller to mutate the live key material in place.

issues.title: Integer overflow in tree size allocation
issues.problem: The tree size calculation `treeSize = 2u * numLeaves * n` could overflow on 32-bit systems for large trees. While LMS_MAX_HEIGHT is 25, LMS_MAX_PRACTICAL_HEIGHT is 15, and this validation might not be enforced in all code paths reaching HbsLmsTree_GenerateAuthPath.

issues.title: Integer overflow in tree size allocation
issues.problem: The tree size calculation `treeSize = 2 * numLeaves * ctx->n` could overflow on 32-bit systems. While there's a height limit check in LmsParaInit, direct calls to LmsTree_ComputeRoot might bypass this validation.

issues.title: Multi-level HSS verification depends on hidden caller-supplied parameters
issues.problem: HssTree_Verify parses each LMS sub-signature using `para->levelPara[i].sigLen` from the context, but CRYPT_HSS_SetPubKey only records level 0 from the public key. A verifier importing only a public key cannot validate multi-level signatures unless the caller separately supplies every lower-level LMS/LMOTS parameter out of band. The signature already contains the type words needed to derive each LMS signature length dynamically.

issues.title: Unsafe memory function memset used instead of memset_s
issues.problem: The code uses the unsafe memset function instead of the required memset_s from libboundscheck as per project coding conventions.

issues.title: Unsafe memory function memset used instead of memset_s
issues.problem: The code uses the unsafe memset function instead of the required memset_s from libboundscheck as per project coding conventions.

issues.title: Unsafe memory function memset used instead of memset_s
issues.problem: The code uses the unsafe memset function instead of the required memset_s from libboundscheck as per project coding conventions.

issues.title: Unsafe memory function memset used instead of memset_s
issues.problem: The code uses the unsafe memset function instead of the required memset_s from libboundscheck as per project coding conventions.

issues.title: Integer overflow in HssGetMaxSignatures returns sentinel value that may not be checked
issues.problem: The function calculates total signatures as product of (1<<height) for all levels. It returns UINT64_MAX on overflow, but callers might not check for this sentinel value. Returning UINT64_MAX could be misinterpreted as a valid (though enormous) signature count.

issues.title: HSS context comparison explicitly ignores the master seed
issues.problem: CRYPT_HSS_Cmp compares only the counter and compressed-parameter prefix of the private key and explicitly skips the 32-byte master seed. Two distinct HSS private keys with the same counter therefore compare equal, causing CRYPT_EAL_PkeyCmp() to report false positives. Additionally, the comparison uses memcmp which is not constant-time.

issues.title: Non-constant-time private key comparison
issues.problem: CRYPT_LMS_Cmp uses memcmp to compare private keys. While the comparison includes counter and parameters, the use of memcmp can leak timing information about private key state.

issues.title: Public HSS level limit exceeds what the private-key format can encode
issues.problem: The API advertises HSS_MAX_LEVELS as 8, but the serialized private key stores the whole hierarchy in an 8-byte compressed parameter block and HSS_MAX_COMPRESSED_LEVELS is only 3. Levels 4-8 are accepted by control setup and then fail later during key generation/import, which is a broken contract.
issues.code:
#define HSS_MAX_LEVELS 8  // Maximum hierarchy levels (RFC 8554)
#define HSS_MIN_LEVELS 1  // Minimum hierarchy levels (1 = equivalent to LMS)
...
#define HSS_MAX_COMPRESSED_LEVELS    3     // Maximum levels that fit in compressed format
issues.fix:
#define HSS_MAX_LEVELS 3  // Maximum levels that fit in compressed parameter format
#define HSS_MIN_LEVELS 1  // Minimum hierarchy levels (1 = equivalent to LMS)
#define HSS_MAX_COMPRESSED_LEVELS    HSS_MAX_LEVELS

issues.title: Published LMS/HSS parameter IDs cannot be instantiated
issues.problem: This PR exports H20, H25, and HSS H20 parameter IDs as supported public enums, but LmsParaInit rejects every LMS tree height above LMS_MAX_PRACTICAL_HEIGHT (15). Callers can select official-looking algorithm IDs that the implementation will always reject at runtime.
issues.code:
CRYPT_LMS_SHA256_H20_W4 = BSL_CID_LMS_SHA256_H20_W4,
CRYPT_LMS_SHA256_H25_W4 = BSL_CID_LMS_SHA256_H25_W4,
...
CRYPT_LMS_SHA256_H20_W2 = BSL_CID_LMS_SHA256_H20_W2,
CRYPT_LMS_SHA256_H20_W8 = BSL_CID_LMS_SHA256_H20_W8,
CRYPT_HSS_SHA256_L2_H20_H20 = BSL_CID_HSS_SHA256_L2_H20_H20,
issues.fix:
/* Only publish parameter sets that the current implementation accepts. */
CRYPT_LMS_SHA256_H5_W4 = BSL_CID_LMS_SHA256_H5_W4,
CRYPT_LMS_SHA256_H10_W4 = BSL_CID_LMS_SHA256_H10_W4,
CRYPT_LMS_SHA256_H15_W4 = BSL_CID_LMS_SHA256_H15_W4,
CRYPT_LMS_SHA256_H10_W2 = BSL_CID_LMS_SHA256_H10_W2,
CRYPT_LMS_SHA256_H15_W2 = BSL_CID_LMS_SHA256_H15_W2,
CRYPT_LMS_SHA256_H10_W8 = BSL_CID_LMS_SHA256_H10_W8,
CRYPT_LMS_SHA256_H15_W8 = BSL_CID_LMS_SHA256_H15_W8,
CRYPT_HSS_SHA256_L2_H10_H10 = BSL_CID_HSS_SHA256_L2_H10_H10,
CRYPT_HSS_SHA256_L2_H15_H15 = BSL_CID_HSS_SHA256_L2_H15_H15,
CRYPT_HSS_SHA256_L3_H10_H10_H10 = BSL_CID_HSS_SHA256_L3_H10_H10_H10,

issues.title: BaseB function has potential integer overflow
issues.problem: The BaseB function can overflow the `o` variable when processing input data. The calculation `o = (o << BYTE_BITS) + x[xi]` can overflow if `b` is large and multiple bytes are processed. While for W=16 (4-bit chunks) this is unlikely, the function doesn't validate its inputs.
